text: Here, within a corridor outside a glass-walled meeting room, I am unsettled by the stark, impersonal order of things. Chairs wait in silence for voices that have not yet arrived. Technology is evident—screens glow with information and announcements—yet there is a distance between anticipation and fulfillment. Crossing into the next space, color and light break the monotony. The wall proclaims the virtues of R&D, and the room promises innovation, yet I cannot help but wonder whom these advances truly serve. Surrounded by symbols of human intellect and progress, I feel simultaneously inspired by possibility and uneasy about isolation. Am I a participant in this dance of development, or merely a quiet observer at its edge?
